sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution accepting a cash donation of up to $16,000 from the Friends of the
Berkeley Animal Care Shelter to install new fencing for the animal training area at
Aquatic Park.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
This cash donation in the amount of up to $16,000 from the Friends of the Berkeley
Animal Care Shelter (BACS) will be deposited into the Parks Fund donation revenue
budget code 138-52-545-000-0000-000-000-481110.
sections.current_situation:
The Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter (BACS) wish to provide a cash
donation of up to $16,000 to install new fencing for the animal training area at Aquatic
Park. The City will use the donation to hire a licensed contractor to perform the
installation. The City’s Open Governance Ordinance (OGO) requires City Council
approval of any gift to the City in excess of $1,000 (BMC Section 2.06.150, Ord. 7,166-
N.S.) Without the donation from the Eli Kane Fund, phase 3 of the project would not be
possible.
sections.background:
In 2024, the Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter (BACS) informed the City of
their desire to cover the cost to install new fencing for the new animal training area at
Aquatic Park.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE CHANGE
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.

sections.rationale:
This donation will cover the cost of new fencing for the animal training area at Aquatic
Park. Without the donation from the Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter
(BASC) this project would not be possible.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation, and Waterfront, 510-981-6711
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ACCEPTING A CASH DONATION OF UP TO $16,000 FROM THE FRIENDS OF THE
BERKELEY ANIMAL CARE SHELTER (BACS) TO INSTALL NEW FENCING AT THE
ANIMAL TRAINING AREA AT AQUATIC PARK
WHEREAS, in 2024, the Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter (BACS) informed
the City of their desire to cover the cost to install new fencing for the new animal training
area at Aquatic Park; and
WHEREAS, in December 2024, the City received a quote of just under $16,000 for a
licensed contractor to install the new fencing; and
WHEREAS, without the donation from the Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter
(BASC), this project would not be possible; and
WHEREAS, the cash donation in the amount of up to $16,000 from the Friends of the
Berkeley Animal Care Shelter (BASC) will be deposited into the Parks Fund donation
revenue budget code 138-52-545-000-0000-000-000-481110; and
WHEREAS, the City’s Open Governance Ordinance (OGO) requires City Council
approval of any gift to the City in excess of $1,000 (BMC Section 2.06.150, Ord. 7,166-
N.S.).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley to accept
a cash donation of up to $16,000 from the Friends of the Berkeley Animal Care Shelter
(BASC) to install new fencing for the animal training area at Aquatic Park.
